data.title: Tesla Stock (TSLA) Climbs as "Musk Metropolis" Merger Talks Gather Pace
data.description:
Shares in EV maker Tesla (TSLA) were higher today as analysts talked up the prospect of a super "Musk Metropolis" merger between Elon Musk's SpaceX and xAI companies.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Merger Mania

It was reported yesterday that SpaceX, home to rockets and Starlink satellites, as well as the X social media platform and xAI's Grok chatbot could come under one company ahead of a major public offering planned for later this year.

Under the proposed deal, xAI shareholders would receive SpaceX shares, and two new entities have already been created in Nevada to help facilitate the transaction. However, the exact value, timing, and structure of the merger are still unclear, and no final agreement has been signed.

The prospect is a tantalizing one given the complementary nature of the technologies involved. Through xAI, Musk is building a massive AI supercomputer called Colossus and could use SpaceX rockets to put AI data centers in space.
data.author: David Craik

data.title: Why Did TechCreate Stock (TCGL) Rocket Again Today?
data.description:
TechCreate (TCGL) stock rocketed again on Friday after the company's shares underwent a massive rally on Thursday. The most interesting thing about this is that the technology consultancy and software solutions company has no idea why its shares are up.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Here's TechCreate's official stance on the recent rally:

"In response to an inquiry from NYSE American regarding recent trading activity in its ordinary shares, the Company stated that it is not aware of any material nonpublic information that has not been publicly disclosed that would account for the recent trading activity."

TechCreate also noted that it doesn't comment on market speculation or fluctuations in its share price. Instead, the company said it will focus on "executing its business strategy and creating long-term shareholder value."

TechCreate Stock Movement Today

TechCreate stock was up 138.03% in pre-market trading on Friday, following an 889.23% rally yesterday. The company's stock has rallied 1,560.77% year-to-date and 2,001.22% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of TCGL stock, as more than 298,000 shares changed hands. Investors will also note that the stock experienced heightened trading activity yesterday, when some 4.94 million shares were traded.
data.author: William White

data.title: Tesla Stock Rallies as Analysts and CEO Agree the Company Is a Tech Powerhouse
data.description:
Tesla (TSLA) shares climbed to $416.56 today, a price action that reflects a major change in how the market calculates the company's value. While a stock price increase is usually just a sign of demand, analysts noted that this specific rally follows a structural shift in Tesla's business model.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

The announcement that flagship luxury car programs will end to make room for robots has forced investors to stop comparing Tesla to companies like Ford (F). Instead, the market is beginning to value it as an artificial intelligence powerhouse. This repricing is why the stock remains at a $1.5 trillion valuation despite selling fewer cars than in previous years.

Musk Retires Flagship Cars to Make Way for Robots

During the earnings call on Wednesday, CEO Elon Musk made the surprise announcement that Tesla will stop producing its high-end Model S and Model X vehicles in the second quarter of 2026. Musk described the decision to end these programs as an "honorable discharge" for the cars that helped build the brand.

Instead of luxury sedans and SUVs, Tesla will turn those production lines at its Fremont, California plant into a massive factory for Optimus, the company's humanoid robot. Musk said the goal is to eventually build "one million units a year" of these robots.

Wall Street Shifts Tesla's Valuation to Physical AI

This massive shift in strategy has prompted major banks to rethink how they price the stock. Analysts from Barclays noted that while cars are still the core business for now, the end of the S and X models marks a symbolic hand-off to what they call Physical AI. Tesla intends to spend $20 billion this year on autonomous driving, robotics, and energy storage to fund this transition—more than double what it spent in 2025.

The shift toward providing "transportation-as-a-service" through the Robotaxi fleet replaces the old focus on selling cars to individuals. This massive technological bet is the reason Tesla now trades at 194 times its future earnings, a multiple that is six times higher than the average for the other major tech companies in the Magnificent Seven.

Skeptics Warn of High Risks and Low Cash Flow

While the stock price is rising, not everyone is convinced the move will be smooth. JPMorgan analyst Ryan Brinkman maintained a sell-equivalent rating, advising "extreme caution" for investors. He pointed out that doubling the spending on robots while not promising to grow car deliveries could hurt the company's ability to generate cash in the next two years. Additionally, while the vision for a robot-filled future is exciting, the automotive segment still brought in 87% of Tesla's revenue in 2025, and it could be years before robots or self-driving taxis actually start making a profit.

Is Tesla a Buy, Hold, or Sell?

According to TipRanks, Tesla stock (TSLA) has received a Hold consensus rating, with 11 Buys, 12 Holds, and six Sells assigned in the last three months. The average 12-month TSLA stock price target is $401.27, suggesting a potential downside of 3.7% from the current level.
data.author: Annika Masrani

data.title: CVX Earnings: Chevron Stock Wobbles as Profit Drop Clouds Q4 Beat
data.description:
Chevron's (CVX) shares were little changed after the American oil supermajor posted fourth-quarter results that slightly beat estimates but fell sharply from a year ago.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

In the final quarter of 2025, Chevron saw adjusted earnings per share of $1.52 and revenue of $46.87 billion, narrowly topping analyst consensus EPS of $1.44 and revenue of $46.66 billion. Both numbers fell 26% and 10% year-over-year, respectively.

In addition, the company's profits shrank by 14% from a year ago to $2.8 billion despite expanded oil production in the quarter.
data.author: Solomon Oladipupo

data.title: U.S. Stock Futures Slide, Investors Digest Trump's Fed Chair Nomination
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fell on Friday morning following a decline in the S&P 500 Index (SPX) and the Nasdaq Composite in the previous trading session, as tech giant Microsoft's (MSFT) Q2 FY26 results failed to impress Wall Street. Meanwhile, investors digested President Donald Trump's announcement of the nomination of Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair, succeeding Jerome Powell. Futures on the Nasdaq 100 (NDX), the S&P 500 (SPX), and the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) were down 0.72%, 0.54%, and 0.52%, respectively, at 8:40 a.m. EST on January 30.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

During Thursday's regular trading, the three major indexes closed mixed. The S&P 500 closed down 0.13%, the tech-focused Nasdaq Composite dropped 0.72%, and the Dow Jones Industrial Average gained 0.11%.

Turning to trending stocks, Apple (AAPL) exceeded fiscal first-quarter expectations, backed by robust iPhone sales. The company expects revenue to grow year over year between 13% and 16% in Q2 FY26. Meanwhile, SanDisk (SNDK) stock rallied 23% after the storage device maker beat Q2 FY26 estimates and issued solid guidance.

Deckers Outdoor (DECK) stock jumped 13% after the maker of Ugg Boots and Hoka sneakers announced better-than-expected 2026 guidance. In contrast, KLA Corp. (KLAC) stock declined 8.1% after the semiconductor equipment maker's fiscal third-quarter guidance failed to impress investors.

Stay ahead of macro events with our up-to-the-minute Economic Calendar — filter by impact, country, and more.
data.author: Sirisha Bhogaraju

data.title: China Lets DeepSeek Buy Nvidia H200 Chips, but NVDA Stock Falls
data.description:
China has reportedly granted its leading AI startup, DeepSeek, approval to purchase Nvidia's (NVDA) H200 AI chips. Reuters, citing sources familiar with the matter, reported that the regulatory conditions are still being finalized. Despite the approval, NVDA is down 1.71% in early trading on Friday as investors await the exact terms and how the deal will benefit Nvidia.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Nvidia Gears Up for China AI Market

Earlier this week, Reuters reported that ByteDance, Alibaba (BABA), and Tencent (TCEHY) have been permitted to purchase more than 400,000 H200 chips in total. According to the latest reports, China's industry and commerce ministries have approved all four companies to purchase H200 chips, with the rules being set by the National Development and Reform Commission (NDRC).

Meanwhile, Nvidia CEO Jensen Huang told reporters in Taipei on Thursday that his company had not received this information and believes that China is still finalizing the licenses.

What This Could Mean for Nvidia?

Nvidia's H200, the company's second-most powerful AI chip, has become a key issue in U.S.-China relations. While Chinese companies want the chip and the U.S. has approved exports, Beijing has been slow to allow imports, which has limited shipments. Notably, Chinese authorities still have the final say on whether the chips can actually be imported.

If China allows H200 chip imports, Nvidia could see a big jump in sales. Demand for the H200 is strong, and shipments to Chinese companies like DeepSeek could add significantly to revenue. Good news on approvals could also boost NVDA stock, but political or regulatory concerns could cause short-term swings.

At the same time, any H200 purchases by DeepSeek could face scrutiny from U.S. lawmakers. For instance, yesterday Congress raised concerns that Nvidia may have helped China's DeepSeek develop AI models that could be used by the military. The committee said Nvidia engineers worked with DeepSeek to improve algorithms, software, and hardware together. However, Nvidia denied all the allegations.

Is Nvidia a Buy or Sell Stock?

According to TipRanks, NVDA stock has a Strong Buy consensus rating based on 38 Buys, one Hold, and one Sell assigned in the last three months. At $262.79, the Nvidia average share price target implies a 36.51% upside potential.
data.author: Kirti Tak

data.title: Why Is Fat Brands Stock Up Today?
data.description:
Fat Brands (FAT) stock underwent a massive rally on Friday despite news that the restaurant chain's shares will be delisted from the Nasdaq. A filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) revealed that the shares will be delisted from the stock exchange at the opening of business on February 4, 2026. This covers the company's Class A, Class B, and Series B Cumulative Preferred Stock.

Claim 50% Off TipRanks Premium

  • Unlock hedge fund-level data and powerful investing tools for smarter, sharper decisions
  • Stay ahead of the market with the latest news and analysis and maximize your portfolio's potential

Fat Brands said that it won't appeal the delisting. Instead, the company expects its shares to start trading on the Pink Limited Market operated by the OTC Markets Group. The company warned investors that the OTC Market offers less liquidity than the Nasdaq and the switch to it could be a negative catalyst for FAT stock.

Fat Brands stock is being delisted from the Nasdaq due to its recent voluntary bankruptcy filing. This filing made it non-compliant with Nasdaq Listing Rules 5101, 5110(b), and IM-5101-1. Considering the bankruptcy filing was made earlier this week, investors in FAT stock were likely expecting the delisting notice.

Fat Brands Stock Movement Today

Fat Brands stock was up 72.46% in pre-market trading on Friday, following a 13.23% fall yesterday. The shares have fallen 29.81% year-to-date and 94.11% over the past 12 months.

With today's news came heavy trading of FAT stock, as more than 95 million shares changed hands, compared to a three-month daily average trading volume of about 61,000 units.
data.author: William White

data.title: Palantir Technologies (PLTR) Q4 Earnings on Deck: Options Market Braces for a 10.70% Swing
data.description:
Palantir Technologies (PLTR) is scheduled to announce its results for the fourth quarter of 2025 on Monday, February 2, and the options market is expecting a volatile reaction. Based on options pricing, traders are expecting a 10.70% move in either direction in PLTR stock following its Q4 2025 earnings. This implied move is slightly higher than the stock's average post-earnings move (in absolute terms) of 9.28% over the past three quarters.
